card_description: Apollo (Greek (uncertain): "Destroyer or shepherd") is a divine or mythological figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Apollo is god of light and prophecy; delphic oracle. Referenced in PKD's discussions of prophecy and illumination. Appears in 3 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Apollo. Context: "Dick explores the "Daimon of Socrates" (verified by Rollo May) as the source of his 1974 "Voice" and Attic Greek."
